response.justification: Sarah Smith has broad full-stack and GIS-adjacent experience, including an open-source geospatial platform (GISCollective) and high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic, which are tangentially relevant. However, the role's core requirement is modern C++ or Rust for systems-level routing pipeline engineering, and her C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 writing IDE components — not modern, production-grade systems C++. Her current and recent work is almost entirely TypeScript/Node.js. There is no routing tile, navigation guidance, or map data transformation experience, and no Rust experience whatsoever, making the technical gap too wide for a mid-senior systems role.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain exposure: founded GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform), built GIS web apps for Green Map, and presented at FOSS4G 2019
Demonstrated high-performance distributed systems work at Sociomantic — low-latency DLang services handling millions of requests/second with async I/O and high-performance databases
Strong engineering culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based dev) and broad Linux/infrastructure background across multiple roles
concerns: C++ experience is dated (2014–2015, NI LabWindows components) and does not reflect modern systems C++ required for production routing pipelines
No Rust experience; primary production languages for the last decade are TypeScript, JavaScript, and DLang — none acceptable substitutes for the role
Zero routing tile, navigation guidance, or map data transformation experience; GIS work is collection/display-focused, not pipeline or tiling-focused
